contract_checks: Fixture inputs/results use contractSchemaVersion from packages/contracts.
Fixture result statuses, markets, and failure reasons are checked against shared contract runtime vocabulary.
Raw metadata snapshots remain separate from normalized FixtureCollectorResult output.
Rate-limited and unsupported-market outcomes are represented as typed PartialFailure fixtures.
risks: Fixtures are examples only; real marketplace crawling remains out of scope.
Future WBS slices must keep fixture domains synthetic and keep session/cookie/token handling disabled.

core_validation_summary: CorePipelineInput keeps fixture collector inputs, sanitized raw snapshots, and normalized FixtureCollectorResult values in separate fields.
normalizeFixtureCollectorResult, validateCorePipelineInput, validateCorePipelineResult, and runDeterministicSourcingPipeline reject invalid fixture shapes with typed error envelopes.

CorePipelineResult now includes deterministic status and sourcing-job-result-summary fields plus typed progress/log/failure events across collect, normalize, filter, dedupe, enrich, image_search_ready, save_ready, and summarize.
risks: Fixture-only validation consumes WBS-13 examples; live crawling, browser automation, session/cookie/token handling, credential handling, and external APIs remain out of scope.
Core package tests use a test-local Node ESM resolver for the existing extensionless workspace source import in built core output.

items.title: Implement fixture-only sourcing job API boundary
items.rollback: Revert this slice and restore prior generated files/backups if validation fails.
items.evidence: Record changed files, commands, PASS/FAIL output, API boundary summary, blockers, remaining risks, and rollback note.

completed_evidence.summary: Implemented fixture-only sourcing job create, status, and result API boundaries using WBS-12 envelope vocabulary, WBS-13 fixture data, and WBS-14-style deterministic core output.
completed_evidence.validation: cd apps/api && pytest PASS; pnpm --filter @project/contracts typecheck PASS; pnpm --filter @project/core typecheck PASS; pnpm --filter @project/collectors test PASS; validate-planning PASS; validate-dev-flow PASS; pnpm validate:all PASS; git diff --check PASS
api_route_summary: POST /api/v1/sourcing/jobs creates the deterministic fixture-backed job and returns SourcingJobCreatedResponse inside ApiResponseEnvelope.
GET /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id} returns completed fixture job status, progress, result summary, retry state, and typed sourcing job errors.
GET /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id}/result returns deterministic core-pipeline-result output with progress/log/failure events and preserved partial failures.
Invalid job IDs and unsupported source types return typed ErrorEnvelope responses.
completed_evidence.fixture_only_boundary: The API reads only the synthetic WBS-13 deterministic fixture JSON and constructs deterministic WBS-14-style core output in memory; it does not crawl marketplaces, call external APIs, automate browsers, handle login/session/cookie/token material, or store secrets.
risks: Tests remain direct handler tests to avoid hiding the carried ASGI/TestClient hang risk from earlier API work.
The job store is deterministic in-memory fixture state only; persistence/background workers remain deferred.
completed_evidence.rollback: Revert the WBS-15 API route/test and planning evidence changes to restore the pre-WBS-15 state.

Unsupported live source behavior is disabled and visibly rejected without implying real marketplace access.

Visible sensitive field names were removed from rendered UI copy while preserving local-only boundary semantics.

items.title: Document local-only browser session handoff boundaries
items.rollback: Revert this slice and restore prior generated files/backups if validation fails.
items.evidence: Record changed files, commands, PASS/FAIL output, browser/session handoff boundary summary, forbidden behavior list, future approval gates, blockers, remaining risks, and rollback note.

completed_evidence.summary: Documented WBS-18 local-only browser session handoff boundaries as design-only guidance for future controlled product phases.
completed_evidence.validation: python -S tools/codex/codex_skillset_generator.py validate-planning --root . PASS; python -S tools/codex/codex_skillset_generator.py validate-dev-flow --root . PASS; node tools/checks/cleanroom-audit.mjs PASS; pnpm validate:all PASS; git diff --check PASS
browser_session_handoff_boundary_summary: The current product phase remains fixture-only across API, web, extension, collectors, and core.
Browser/session handoff is documented as a future human-approved, same-device, metadata-only concept.
WBS-18 adds no implementation code, dependencies, permissions, runtime configuration, browser automation, marketplace access, live crawling, external API calls, or session extraction.
forbidden_behavior: No Playwright, Selenium, Puppeteer, browser drivers, login automation, captcha solving, anti-bot bypassing, marketplace access, live crawling, or external API calls.

No reading, exporting, persisting, logging, committing, or transmitting cookies, sessions, tokens, credentials, local storage, browser profiles, account data, service-account files, API keys, passwords, or private keys.
No validator, clean-room audit, unsupported-source error, or fixture-only behavior weakening.
future_approval_gates: A later approved WBS must name exact markets, user action, data categories, retention policy, and rollback owner before any implementation.
Security, privacy, anti-bot compliance, contract, test, clean-room, secret scanning, dependency, permission, and rollback evidence must pass before enabling any future implementation.
risks: This is design-only documentation; implementation remains deferred and unsupported live/manual sources continue to be rejected.
Future phases must prove that secret/session material remains metadata-only and never enters DTOs, logs, fixtures, artifacts, or git history.
completed_evidence.rollback: Revert the WBS-18 architecture, contract docs, phase gate, command queue, and manifest evidence changes to restore the pre-WBS-18 state.

completed_evidence.validation: pnpm validate:all PASS; validate-planning PASS; validate-dev-flow PASS; workspace-check PASS; cleanroom-audit PASS; git diff --check PASS
fixture_only_integration_smoke_summary: WBS-12 shared sourcing job contracts provide fixture-safe request, status, result, error, progress, and envelope vocabulary.
WBS-13 deterministic collector fixtures and WBS-14 core validation preserve synthetic fixture provenance, raw/normalized separation, deterministic output, and partial failures.
WBS-15 API, WBS-16 web UI, and WBS-17 extension readiness consume the same fixture-only vocabulary while rejecting unsupported live/manual sources.
WBS-18 browser/session handoff remains local-only design documentation with no implementation.
clean_room_boundary_assertions: WBS-19 changed planning/evidence docs only and did not modify product runtime code.
No marketplace access, live crawling, browser automation, login/session/cookie/token handling, credential handling, secrets, or external API calls were added.
Clean-room audit and validators remain enabled and passing.
completed_evidence.fixture_provenance: The integrated smoke path uses WBS-13 synthetic clean-room fixtures with reserved fixture.invalid URLs and WBS-14 through WBS-17 deterministic fixture vocabulary; WBS-19 adds no new fixture data or live marketplace responses.
risks: The WBS-05 full ASGI/TestClient smoke risk remains documented because TestClient hung in the earlier local environment.

Future live marketplace, browser/session, automation, or external network behavior remains unapproved and requires a later WBS with security, privacy, anti-bot, contract, test, clean-room, secret-scanning, dependency/permission, and rollback evidence.
completed_evidence.next_rollout_decision: Proceed only to a separately approved product slice; WBS-19 does not approve live marketplace, browser/session, automation, or external network behavior.
completed_evidence.rollback: Revert the WBS-19 evidence document, phase gate, command queue, compact command queue JSON, and manifest changes to restore the pre-WBS-19 planning state.

items.title: Define local runtime execution policy and fixture-to-runtime promotion gates
items.rollback: Revert this slice and restore prior generated files/backups if validation fails.
items.evidence: Record changed files, commands, PASS/FAIL output, local runtime policy summary, fixture-to-runtime promotion gates, clean-room boundary assertions, remaining risks, and rollback note.

completed_evidence.summary: Defined the controlled local runtime execution policy and fixture-to-runtime promotion gates before opening runtime product code.
completed_evidence.validation: validate-planning PASS; validate-dev-flow PASS; cleanroom-audit PASS; pnpm validate:all PASS; git diff --check PASS
local_runtime_policy_summary: Fixture-only behavior remains the current default and synthetic/sanitized fixtures remain the only collector evidence source.

Later WBS items may introduce local persisted state, local API lifecycle behavior, web/local API wiring, extension/local API readiness, and local audit records only after explicit promotion gates pass.
Runtime implementation must remain local-only until a later go/no-go decision.
fixture_to_runtime_promotion_gates: Contract gate: shared DTOs, API envelopes, extension messages, and lifecycle errors are documented and validated.
Fixture gate: deterministic fixture tests pass with provenance intact.
Local persistence gate: persisted files contain no secrets, credentials, cookies, sessions, tokens, passwords, browser profiles, account data, marketplace responses, or external API data.
Runtime audit gate: lifecycle actions produce local clean-room audit events excluding secret/session material.
UI/API gate: web and API behavior remains deterministic under tests.
Extension boundary gate: extension code cannot access cookies, sessions, tokens, local storage, credentials, browser profiles, account data, or marketplace pages.
Integration gate: local-only integration smoke passes with clean-room audit passing.
Go/no-go gate: live collection remains blocked until the WBS-28 decision explicitly approves a later limited boundary.
clean_room_boundary_assertions: WBS-20 is planning/evidence only and does not implement runtime product code, API runtime actions, a persisted job store, live crawling, marketplace access, browser automation, login automation, credential/session/cookie/token capture, secrets, or external API calls.
No validators, tests, clean-room audit, unsupported-source guards, or safety boundaries were weakened.
The WBS-05 full ASGI/TestClient deferred-smoke risk remains preserved.
risks: WBS-21 through WBS-24 will introduce local runtime behavior and must prove local-only execution with no external IO.
WBS-25 must remain a stub and must not read browser state or capture credential/session material.
WBS-28 is a decision gate only and must not enable live collection by itself.
completed_evidence.rollback: Revert the WBS-20 policy evidence, boundary, phase gate, command queue, compact command queue JSON, manifest, and PLANS.md changes to restore the pre-WBS-20 planning state.

completed_evidence.validation: cd apps/api && pytest PASS; validate-planning PASS; validate-dev-flow PASS; cleanroom-audit PASS; pnpm validate:all PASS; git diff --check PASS
local_persisted_store_summary: Added LocalSourcingJobStore with load_jobs, save_jobs, create_job, get_job, update_job_status, get_job_result, and reset_store_for_tests helpers.
Persisted state writes a versioned local JSON document to AUTOMETA_JOB_STORE_PATH for tests or .runtime/sourcing-jobs.json by default.
API create/status/result behavior remains fixture-only and reconstructs deterministic core output from synthetic fixture data.
forbidden_fields: real external URLs beyond reserved fixture/synthetic URLs
tests: API tests cover persisted create/reload, read after reload, status update persistence, deterministic result summary, invalid job ID typed error, secret-like key rejection, local test path isolation, and existing fixture-only API behavior.
risks: This is a local JSON store only; concurrent writes, migrations, and production-grade locking remain deferred.
WBS-22 will add lifecycle actions and must preserve the local-only/no-external-IO boundary.
completed_evidence.rollback: Revert the WBS-21 API store, test fixture, persistence tests, evidence, planning, and .gitignore changes to restore the pre-WBS-21 state.

items.title: Add API job lifecycle actions: create, read, cancel, retry
items.rollback: Revert this slice and restore prior generated files/backups if validation fails.
items.evidence: Record changed files, commands, PASS/FAIL output, API lifecycle summary, typed error/cancel/retry evidence, remaining risks, and rollback note.

completed_evidence.summary: Expanded the local-only sourcing job API lifecycle with create/read/cancel/retry actions backed by the WBS-21 persisted store.
api_lifecycle: POST /api/v1/sourcing/jobs creates a persisted fixture-backed local job.
POST /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id}/cancel cancels queued/running local fixture jobs only.
POST /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id}/retry retries failed/cancelled local fixture jobs only.
GET /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id}/result preserves deterministic fixture/core result output.
status_transitions: completed is not cancellable or retryable
risks: The persisted store remains a local JSON store; concurrent writes, migrations, and production-grade locking are deferred.
Web and extension runtime wiring remains deferred to WBS-23 and WBS-24.
The WBS-05 ASGI/TestClient deferred-smoke risk remains preserved.
completed_evidence.rollback: Revert the WBS-22 API lifecycle routes/helpers, lifecycle tests, API contract docs, local-runtime evidence, phase gate, command queue, and manifest changes to restore the WBS-21 state.

Cancel uses POST /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id}/cancel only for queued/running states.
Retry uses POST /api/v1/sourcing/jobs/{job_id}/retry only for failed/cancelled states.
Deterministic fixture/core result summaries remain visible.
risks: Full browser interaction smoke remains deferred.
